reviews.review_title: Great throw back to late 2000 era games
reviews.review_description:
This game has a decent storyline , haven’t got too far but it reminds me if Warhammer Battle Sister was actually a good game.

The combat is tough as nails why I haven’t got very far but it’s fun and fair.

Graphics are immersive enough. Nothing too fancy and it’s not bad. Suitable for the Quest. 

It reminds me of one of those 2008 type of shooters . Back when FPS were at its best.

Best of all. It’s super cheap price.

I love the atmosphere. The gameplay is easy to learn but very hard to master. On easy I had to use godmode several times. Without godmode I would could not have played it, with it, it was perfect. Please release part two soon!

I followed this game since it was first announced, being initially lured in by the various narrative talents from some of my favourite games being on board. When the initial reviews hit, I decided to wait for a sale. So I did and now here I am. I was extremely surprised at how much I enjoy this game. A lot of people aren't too pleased with the graphics, but I must say that aside from some oddly out of scale ephemera (giant ashtrays and gargantuan ciggies?) I actually  really like them: They look A LOT better in the headset than in screenshots, having a great sense of depth and a more convincing 3D  effect than other games with a higher level of detail. The first time that I saw a humanoid figure in the distance, I was impressed by how it walked. It's true that dynamic shadows aren't really a thing here and that this dev doesn't have the kind of budget that allows for the kind of detail and realism that you'd see in, say, Metro for example, but the graphics here have a style of their own which is clean, convincing and plays with different visual styles  for each hulk that you board. The first ship is very Bioshock/Systemshock  in appearance, with a touch of Metroid, the next one has a more Weyland Yutani vibe and so on.  The gameplay is classic survival horror done properly. The way the genre has been implemented in a game with infinite ammo that still feels like survival horror is quite clever. It's really tough, but mostly, a healthily paranoid approach to this game will keep you alive. Mostly. There's some great  voice acting here, a cool anthology of stories presented in a way that vaguely reminds me of a binned concept for Deadspace 4, with all tales written by a bunch of people responsible for some of the best survival horror game scripts of all time. The dev(s?) capitalises on this with some truly clever narrative moments integrated into the actual gameplay that made me smile and feel like I was in a movie. Out of space, but sounds are great too.

reviews.review_title: Dont let the gfx fool you
reviews.review_description: This is an awesome horror comedy type of bullet hell with great story. The graphics may look low poly but its part of the charm, glad i picked this one up.

This is a great game, hugely underrated, I got in on sale, and if you can - don't hesitate! Worth full price. As others have mentioned the screenshots don't do the game justice, it looks way better in the headset. So great to see original world building in VR with fresh ideas and super fun fps gameplay to boot.

reviews.review_title: Love this game let's you be invincible
reviews.review_description: At first I was hating it for being too hard, even on easy mode. Then I discovered you can enable "invulnerability", which gave me a chance to enjoy the game. Love the story, atmosphere, music, voice 'acting' (AI voices I believe). Even with vulnerability enabled I got very scared, and no game so far has ever accomplished that, perhaps aside from Paradise Hotel. Without invulnerability enabled, I was screaming non stop because of the spiders. Then I also discovered you can stomp on them by pressing 'B' on the controller. It's very dark and you can't see the aliens half of the time until they're on you. I don't know how anyone can go through the lasers without the invulnerability mode on. Or escape all the swarm of aliens in some parts. And I'm usually good in FPS, but without invulnerability I would not have been able to play this. I have seen some disadvantages reviews of this game, I beg to differ and think this is thoroughly enjoyable, switched on a full range of emotions.

I played with indestructible mode and had a great time!
you explore different spaceships that are infested with aliens. your gun is both rifle and shotgun, ammo can be thrown like a grenade. each ship is different and has its own story.
finished the game today!

reviews.review_title: Does exactly what it sets out to do!
reviews.review_description:
For those who like a challenge this game succeeds almost completely with its intentions.

The atmosphere is fantastic, with a creeping sense of dread as you explore the corridors of the spacestations. This is helped by excellent voice acting from the cast, and some very strong writing. 

The graphics remind me of the original half-life one on PC combined with the horror of Dead Space. While not the flashiest, they are solid with a good resolution on my quest 3 and excellent performance.

There are a few flaws, unsurprising for such a small developer, grenades feels very floaty, sometimes it can be unclear from which direction monsters are attacking as they slowly drain your health. And there are bugs, as a left-handed player I was initially unable to use the rifle without it shooting me however after messaging the developer this bug was quickly fixed allowing me to play the game properly.

Even with these bugs, if you read the description of the game and think it's for you I would highly recommend it!

reviews.review_title: Indie gem punching above its weight
reviews.review_description: The atmosphere is great in this game and the voice acting is absolutely stellar. Seriously, it's movie quality. Have no clue what people the developer knows or what favors he called in for work of that quality.
True Great .Love it. Well done .

It baffles me that people say this game looks good. Almost no lighting effects, blurry textures, models and environments that lack details - this game looks like a Half-Life 1 mod!
At first I thought the game might make up for it with good gameplay, but the combat is dull and repetitive, the "puzzles" are boring and only a few objects are interactable, so you don't really want to explore your surroundings either.
The story might be good, dialogue is written pretty well. But I was so bored by the game already in the first level that it is hard to keep playing.
Great simple space alien shooter game with a good story. Enjoyed it!

reviews.review_title: Worth the price of entry 3 times over
reviews.review_description: Bought this for 10 pounds expecting a half  decent indie horror game and was pleasantly surprised to get what I can only call a AAA title! Sure some of the voice work is a little wonky but the care put into the story shown through holo logs like horizon zero dawn and text logs. Not to mention the environmental storytelling, the way the aesthetic changes with each ship you make it to, and that horrible wall of flesh with teeth that chases you I was hooked and only took one break before finishing the game, its a good few hours long and, if the credits are to  be believed, we will be getting more content! Can't recommend this game enough for someone wanting a break from the endless rogue lite games available and would have been worth it at 30 pounds.

Truly an astonishing amazingly gorgeous FPS cosmic action horror wanna play it again and again while kicking alien butt also I love exploring dead ships finding lore on what happened i do hope the story continues so I can explore more ships and kick alien butt thanks for bringing this game to life

PS. I also love/enjoy being my own Marine protagonist truly amazing idea

developer_response.body: Thanks stacfamily100 for the awesome review, super glad you enjoyed it and keep kicking alien butt!

reviews.review_description: The storyline and campaign are great,  I like tje environmental immersion and weapon system.  Where the game lacks is it's controls.  You can't change which joystick to move with. If you don't grip the gun just right you end up shooting yourself and sometimes the controls glitch altogether and you cant move

developer_response.body: Hi TheDouggernaut, sorry you hit that bug with the gun grip. I've just pushed a new bug fix and the gun grip / shooting self issue should now be fixed! Thanks for playing.

reviews.review_description: It won't be for everyone & I'm confident many will write this one off due to the retro visual aesthetic (it looks like a "demake" of Cosmodread), however those who persevere will find a cool indie sci-fi shooter with bullethell elements, Dead Space influences & classic boomer shooter vibes. Updates to implement things like increased smooth turn speed, a sprint button, more weapons, better lighting and some shadows will go a long way to making this game appeal to a wider audience & I hope to see it grow and flourish with time. I think this is the devs 2nd game, there's definitely a lot of promise here in such a young studio.

developer_response.body:
Thanks Beardo, as long time fans of the channel it's incredible to have you play our game! Watching you play Cosmodread way back when was a big inspiration for us to make this in the first place. Updates are cooking and on the way soon, we'll defo get all those suggestions in ASAP!

Update: Patch 1 is now live, including turn speed options, increased movement speed, difficulty options, balance fixes and loads of other goodies. I'm working hard on Patch 2, including lighting and shadow upgrades and smackable toys in the prison section! Cheers!

reviews.review_title: Fun, but stay on your toes
reviews.review_description: Tight lil adventure so far. Hope to see updates and more optimizations 🤙

I got this because they mentioned some really good writers from different games so I was expecting not very much but at least a good story. Then when I actually got to the game everybody was saying that the graphics were not that good. But I think the graphics are amazing and the atmosphere the only thing is that it's kind of hard but it's scary and I like it so far I've only played it for not even not even an hour yet but so far I'm really enjoying it I think it's a spooky and I think it's actually really neat.
This is one of those artistic games runs well get your heartbeat going and I think the story is going to be amazing.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! You are our first 5 star review and it brought a smile to the whole team. Hope you enjoy the rest of the game and keep an eye out for some cool new free DLC we'll be adding soon!

reviews.review_description:
Updated review: the 2 game updates addressed a lot of the issues I had with the game.  Looking forward to future updates.  Changed my stars from 3 to 4.
Pre-ordered because they mentioned dead space, but…
It could really benefit from a couple things:
Change the enemy color/illumination balance so they stand out more against the background as often you just can’t see them except for their glowing eyes.  This combined with not enough enemy Audio clues Makes it difficult to understand when they are approaching you, especially from behind.
Keep working on enemy movement speed and animation so the combat has weight.  Currently it feels somewhat frantic in a way that is out of the players control.
Background ambient sound would be nice for those that don’t want to have the music playing.

There's some good atmosphere, level design and some interesting lore created through imaginative texts, narrative and audio logs in this game. So it has a lot of potential, but it misses the mark in so many ways. 

Graphics are very basic, but that's okay. I'm old-school and acknowledge that great graphics don't make a great game, but developers do need to focus on enjoyable gameplay. 

The issues I have with this game really spoiled what should and could so easily have been a great experience. For instance the walking speed is painfully slow. It's absolutely dreadful. Why? It's a completely unnecessary design choice and serves no purpose in the game other than to frustrate and irritate.
 
The creatures that spawn make virtually no sound. There's a very polite bee-like buzz from some of the flying insects but all too often you can find yourself being attacked from behind by virtually silent spiders that sneaked up on you from an area that you already cleared and then double cleared. Often the barely audible noise that the creatures do make is drowned out by the narrative voices that constantly play in the background. All too often you are killed by a respawn of inaudible small creatures from an area you've already cleared when you're trying to listen to some other information the game is throwing at you. You don't even realise that the respawn is there. It feels cheap and a poor way of artificially making the game hard.

When you shoot a creature it breaks into pieces and those pieces then roll around on the floor for far too long. That doesn't sound like an issue but the developers have chosen to simply rely on throwing hordes of small creatures at you rather than building atmospheric tension with fewer but more aggressive ones, so when there are multiple small things coming at you and similarly sized pieces of dead things rolling around it's often too difficult to distinguish one from the other. 

The variety of creatures is very poor too and you spend a lot of time simply popping shots at insects and spidery-things time after time after time. It's boring.

Gunplay is another disaster. Sometimes the gun appears reasonably accurate, but then the coding appears to lose all ability to place your shot where you know you're pointing the gun. I prove this several times by holding the gun steady at static wall targets. Sometimes it took one shot, yet other times the shots would hit wide but then find the target after 3 or 4 shots when I hadn't moved the gun at all. It's far too hit and miss, pun intended, to be enjoyable. Other games can deliver effective gun accuracy so why isn't it delivered here? 

The developers may have intended this game to feel like an old-school horror shooter, and that's fine with me. Sadly though there is too much reliance on constant spawn and respawn of the same creatures time after time. So much so that I frequently cursed out loud, not due to good horror or jump-scares, but due to feeling cheated by lazy design choice and a lack of imagination. That broke the final thread of immersion that I had left and I lost interest in paying any more. 

I hope the developers work on some of these issues and patch the game to make it as good as it could be. 
As it stands I couldn't recommend this even at a discounted price.

I like the atmosphere, level design and voice acting / story but the combat is way too difficult.   I could only play the game with invulnerability turned on which spoilt the tension somewhat.

For me there were way too many enemies, that just keep on coming, especially when trying to complete a puzzle at the same time.    When the flying enemies reach you they seem to partially clip through you and its hard to work to work out where they are around you and actually shoot them.   Combat was especially difficult in a couple of cases when youre forced to stay in a very small area or it resets progress.

Overall though I did enjoy it and its well worth the price, but the combat could do with some tweaking.   It would be great to add some difficulty settings (easy / normal / hard) and much reduce the spawn rates.

developer_response.body: Hi Tiddles451, thanks for playing and for the feedback, I really appreciate you taking the time! Patch 1 has just gone live, which I hope should address some of your comments; I've added difficulty settings, balanced combat encounters, expanded the combat "hold" areas and added lots of other quality of life stuff. Have more tweaks coming up in Patch 2 (hopefully next week) including varying swarm spawn rates to make combat even better. Cheers and hope you enjoy!

reviews.review_description: As a fan of games that tell a great story, for me By Grit Alone was saved a little by the anthology of stories being so well written and woven through the use of holo recreations and, audio and written logs – with each ships terror being well explained. But, what is a shame with By Grit Alone is the rest of the choices really take away from the experience and the story alone cannot save it. Most elements pull you away from the immersion that is one of the most important elements in VR, especially in the horror genre. What really saved the game for me was the game options allowing you to turn on invincibility – meaning you can work your way through it a lot quicker and remove the frustrations from some of the choices. Which I was happy to see as I would have likely struggled to get through the whole story without this option.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for the time to review and glad you enjoyed the stories; the writers and I had a great time building this universe! The game was too difficult on release which took away from the story for sure, I've now turned down the default difficulty and added a difficulty setting in the last patch, I'm also further fine tuning balance for upcoming patch #2 in a lot of big ways. I'm hoping with a bit of work I can make invincibility not needed for anyone! Also working on lots of quality of life fixes and general upgrades across the board. Cheers and thanks again for playing!

reviews.review_description: This game really prioritises story and atmosphere, making it a huge breath of fresh-air compared with the many pretty-but-empty FPS or battle royale FPS available. If you want to experience a series of foreboding short stories while you shoot things up, this is really the only game in VR to offer it - and I'm grateful it's here!

developer_response.body: Thanks jacktionman, appreciate the great review! Love to hear your enjoying the mix of stories and shooting, I think its a match made in heaven too. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: Feels like Dead Space in VR
reviews.review_description: Amazing stories, each ship you explore has its own weird creepy bonkers thing going on. My favourite is the religious space cult

developer_response.body: Thanks fumikoenchi, I think "weird creepy bonkers" describes it perfectly, hope you enjoy the game!

reviews.review_description:
Game looks great and has a great story. But......there are few enemy variety, enemy respawn, and shooting that has zero impact or weight to it. 

The game is ridiculously difficult to a fault. They don't have difficultly settings but u can make yourself invincible .......lame.  it's so hard it's zero fun and just frustrating. 

It gets the three stars for atmosphere, graphics and story. Loses 2 for difficulty, terrible gun play, lame enemies. I don't know why every other vr programmer decides to put "bees" as enemies in their games. Starship troopers did it too but at least in that game they don't rush you and stick so close that you cant shoot them. Oh when u die, half the time you don't realize bc you can keep shooting while small font says "you died"

Game could of been awesome but at it's current state I don't think I will even finish it.

developer_response.body: Hi Kill3r_Chihulahula, thanks for the review and comment. Totally agree, it came out of the gate a too difficult; I released an update yesterday with a difficult setting and spawn rate changes which is live right now, Patch #2 is also on the way with even more difficulty tweaks and combat changes, coming up ASAP (hopefully next week). I'm changing up spawning to be more dynamic and hopefully making combat feel more weighty and fairer too. Heard you on the VR bees, I'll make sure the next game skips them 😂 Thanks for the kind words about the story, hopefully with the first patch live now and future patches I'm working to get it from 3 stars to 5. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: This is a message to the developers. Thank you for a wonderful story, game, and adventure, I can't wait to see the next chapter of the story. You all got my full support and unconditional love, and if you needed some help, I'm always willing to report a couple of bugs 😇

developer_response.body: Thanks Wolfy_Whitefox for the lovely message, we really appreciate it and are glad you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_description:
The good:
The gun feels good switching from shotgun mode to precision firing.  

The environments also look cool. 

The story seems interesting.

Issues:

Smooth turn is super slow.

No sprint.

When you die the screen fades, but you see yourself still moving around interacting and shooting it's just faded.

The game doesn't have difficulty settings and seems too difficult for casual play.  I want some challenge, but I'm mostly interested in the story, so I'd like to turn the difficulty down, so I can progress easier.

I know this is nitpicky, but I don't like the use of green and blue coloring and ugly font which makes the game look cheap.  I think using a more subtle color palette would make it so the game doesn't look cheap.  For me it hurts the atmosphere I'm in a dark gloomy room and there's this ugly green glowing button.

Summary:

Overall it seems fun with good gun mechanics and interesting story, but it needs faster smooth turning, sprinting, an option for easier difficulty, and a more subtle coloring on things like buttons.

developer_response.body:
Hi AD_Bad, thanks for the really detailed review. We're working on a patch (hopefully out this week) that should have the movement and turn speed changes your looking for. We'll keep working hard on the game and have some cool free DLC in the pipeline so keep an eye out for some more changes really soon. Enjoy and thanks again for playing and reviewing!

Update: I've just release Patch 1, including smooth turn speed options, increased player speed and difficulty options! Thanks again for taking the time to review and I'll keep working on patches, so really appreciate the feedback to make BGA all the better. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: Lots of fun and pretty scary

developer_response.body: Cheers stefunct, glad your enjoying By Grit Alone and thanks for the review!

reviews.review_description: Bought this wanting another Horror shooter and i got what i wanted and some! The gameplay is challenging and frantic and it usually feels like your JUST scraping by. And the story is super intriguing so far. Theres enough pull to keep me playing but it doesnt overshare or overdump lore onto you. Kudos devs!

developer_response.body: Thanks Snacctime, really appreciate the review! Cheers!

reviews.review_description: Brilliant .the game is still juddering turning left and right  .now and again the gameworks well then goes back to being stuck .this is after the update

developer_response.body: Really sorry to hear this! Looking into this now if I can recreate the issue, find the problem and fix it. Please feel free to drop me an email (charlie@crookspeak.com) with any details of the problem and I'll try my best to get it fixed!

reviews.review_description:
Give the writing some time (past tutorial and let yourself wander and see a few rooms) and pay attention to some details. You will feel things of the likes of Bioshock and Dead Space. Makes sense considering writers from Dead Space, RE Village, and Dr. Who did the writing in this game. Have faith and let the writing take you. Unless you die, first!

Five stars.

developer_response.body: Thanks ScytheKarnival, really appreciate the review, glad you are enjoying the writing!

reviews.review_description:
Really enjoyed the combat - lots of enemies all over the floors walls and ceiling makes it VERY intense! 
The graphics aren’t photorealistic BUT I think they nailed the creepy sci-fi atmosphere 🛸Enjoying the story so far, have played about 2 hours and will be going back for more!

developer_response.body: Thanks KDizzle28, keep going with the story and hopefully you'll have a horrific (but funny) time. We've got some shadow updates coming soon to help the visuals pop.

reviews.review_title: An ode to classic space horror
reviews.review_description:
Cool art style which surprises you in key moments. Some scenes and textures can look simple but the next minute you’ve got  genuinely detailed rooms and things to explore. It pays off to look around and find vlogs or read the content - clearly a lot of passion for lore here! Felt very nostalgic

Smoother turning could be implemented but it does give you that sense of fear when you know you can’t just run away from everything - this isn’t COD after all!  

I really enjoyed having to think and actually try when shooting the gun. Too many games make the process too slick and easy when I think it adds genuine panic and fear when you don’t just auto reload or auto aim your way through a game. 

Keep it up guys! Really cool game.

developer_response.body: Cheers Ellibotty, love to hear your having a good time! We've got more smooth turn options coming in our first patch which we'll be dropping shortly!

reviews.review_description:
I do not know what this game is trying to be, in all honesty. Don't get me wrong, it's a good game, and it is more challenging than I had thought before buying it. Combat is functional with a transforming gun. In one hand, it is a shotgun, powerful in close but pointless at a distance. Using both hands turns it into a rife, weaker, but useful for those enemies you want to soften before they get close. The game is actually pretty good.

But, I have no idea whether this game is trying to be an action game or a horror game. In my opinion, it bounces between them too much. I can never tell if I'm meant to be scared or if I am meant to be feared. Not a deal breaker by any means, but definitely something to take note of.

reviews.review_title: Cosmic horror with a lot of promise ☠️
reviews.review_description: The game is pretty rad! I love the frantic combat and cosmic horror vibes. Love all the interesting lore scattered around. Looking forward to the future of this game! Could use more blood spraying when you die. 👻 Keep up the good work! 🤟

developer_response.body: Cheers IndiGhost95, glad your enjoying the game and thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description:
this game appears to have promise, and the developer appeared to be committed. I would give a higher review if two issues were addressed – one implement the difficulty levels so that a player can choose the level of challenge they prefer, and two, if to hold the gun, the grip could be a toggle instead of a hold so that you don’t have to constantly hold down the grip button to keep using the gun. This particularly helps for people who have some arthritis, as I do. Both of these would increase the accessibility of the game and make it fun for a wider variety of folks. Thanks for considering this. 
￼

developer_response.body: Hi jkul777, thanks for reviewing and commenting. I released Patch #1 last Wednesday adding a difficulty setting, it's available in the options menu under gameplay options, for easy, normal, hard and brutal modes! You may just need to update. I'm working hard on more patches to further tweak the diffuiclty so watch this space soon! I hope you enjoy and can rereview! Cheers

reviews.review_description: As a massive fan of the original Deadspace, By Grit Alone really takes me back to the old days of survival with frantic shooting to survive! A must have for sci-fi shooter fans!

developer_response.body: We love DeadSpace too (as you can probably tell). Glad your enjoying it, keep on stomping your way through!

reviews.review_description:
I'm very early on in the game but I'm very impressed. As a fan of horror and sci-fi, this game brings a lot to the table. The atmosphere is dark with strange noises and creatures creeping up out of nowhere. Must be alert at all times cause you never know what's around the corner. I appreciate that. I also enjoy the art style. It has that old school feel to it that just seems to blend well with this type of game. I am also enjoying how the story is unfolding. Besides text logs, you also get to visually see hologram type animations of the encounters of those there before you. I thought that was a great idea. It makes you feel even more connected to the story. Very well done.

The only reason I didn't give it a 5 is due to a couple minor issues. Believe me, these issues are not a big deal and don't deter from the great experience that awaits. My only two issues. 1) the belt that holsters my weapons tends to not stay in place. It's hard to explain. Instead of staying steady I notice my belt will move at times when I'm reaching for my gun. Id prefer if possible to have it more steady to where I know exactly where my weapon is. Especially when the action picks up. 2) Certain spots where I have to push a button it seems the height is a bit off. Makes you feel a bit short at times. Besides those minor issues I'm really impressed and look forward to progressing further.

developer_response.body: Hi Edward, thanks for the kind words and detailed review. I laughed out loud at the height bit, I'm fixing this at the moment for an upcoming patch, but didn't think anyone else had noticed yet! Really glad your enjoying the game, thanks again.
